_provenance.stage: Stage 6 — Synthesis (retroactive rebuild from Codex Stage 3)
version_history: v3 (2026-05-17): retroactive editorial pass. Stage 3 (Codex) FAS=58 with 6 verified claims. 2025 figures updated with MHTC April 2026 release (1.84M travellers in early 2025). FMTH programme status clarified — 4 hospitals were finalists undergoing evaluation as of July 2025, not 'recognised' as the original cards stated. 'Other countries use cross-subsidy' (UNVERIFIED generality) replaced with single verified India example. Reframe-and-analogy claims softened from assertion to policy question.

revision_log: CORRECTED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — '2025 target RM3 billion' updated with verified MHTC April 2026 release: 1.84M healthcare travellers in early 2025.
CORRECTED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — FMTH programme: 4 hospitals 'recognised' → 'shortlisted ... finalists undergoing evaluation' per Stage 3 (July 2025 status).

REPHRASED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED — 'Most medical tourists treated at same private hospitals where senior specialists concentrate' (broad claim) → 'MHTC shortlisted several major hospitals for FMTH; MOH supports public hospitals separately'.
REPHRASED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED — 'Growth targets compete with public healthcare HR needs' (policy inference) → 'raises a workforce-planning question' (frames as question, not asserted outcome).

CORRECTED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED — 'Other countries with active medical tourism use cross-subsidy' (generality not verified) → 'Some jurisdictions use cross-subsidy mechanisms. India's Charitable Hospital obligations...' (only India example actually verified).
REPHRASED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED — 'Malaysia has no equivalent mandate' qualified to 'in the sources reviewed'.

REPHRASED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED — Analogy 'every export patient has an opportunity cost' → 'medical-tourism growth may create workforce trade-offs if it draws on scarce specialist capacity faster than training pipelines refill it' (probabilistic, not asserted).
REPHRASED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED — View 'the fix is...' labelled as 'reform proposal' (editorial recommendation).

PRESERVED (Stage 3 VERIFIED): MHTC RM2.72B 2024 / 1.6M travellers / +14% YoY; RM12B 2030 target; ~4× scale-up to 2030; public hospital specialist shortages; India Charitable Hospital reserved beds; growth target implies specialist-supply question.
